theorems.name: Any false implies none
theorems.description: Helper lemma: if List.any returns false for a predicate, no element in the list satisfies that predicate. Technical machinery connecting boolean List.any computation to propositional reasoning. Not part of the paper's argument; required because the main predicates use Bool computation.

theorems.name: Any true implies exists
theorems.description: Helper lemma: if List.any returns true for a predicate, a witness element exists in the list satisfying that predicate. Technical machinery connecting boolean List.any computation to propositional reasoning. Not part of the paper's argument; required because the main predicates use Bool computation.

theorems.name: Framework contestability lemma
theorems.description: If a primitive is framework-variant, no substrate containing it is neutral. Formalizes Case (2025) Section 4.2: a proposition whose truth conditions depend on interpretive framework conclusions violates either EXT (some admissible framework denies it) or INC (it is framework-variant). Either violation suffices; the proof uses INC as the most direct path.

theorems.name: Neutral if only neutral
theorems.description: Upper bound of the main theorem. If an ontology contains only neutral primitives, it is neutral (satisfies both EXT and INC). Requires neutral_primitives_undisputed; holds only in domains where identity conditions are not themselves contested.

theorems.name: Not neutral if causal or normative
theorems.description: Lower bound of the main theorem. If an ontology contains any causal or normative primitive, it is not neutral. Follows from framework_relativity alone; holds in any domain where causal and normative primitives are contested across admissible frameworks.

theorems.name: Only neutral primitives implies interpretive non-commitment
theorems.description: If an ontology contains only neutral primitives, it satisfies interpretive non-commitment (INC). Establishes that INC follows from neutral_primitives_undisputed alone without requiring a separate axiom. Used as a helper in neutral_if_only_neutral.

theorems.name: Ontological neutrality theorem
theorems.description: Main biconditional result from Case (2025). An ontology is neutral if and only if it contains no causal or normative primitives. The two directions have different epistemic status: the lower bound requires only framework_relativity; the upper bound additionally requires neutral_primitives_undisputed.

theorems.name: Separate stability
theorems.description: A neutral substrate is separately consistent with each of two mutually contradicting frameworks. Formalizes Case (2025) Section 4.1: the substrate's role is common-ground provision, not arbitration. The EXT conjunct of Neutral implies stability under all admissible frameworks, which subsumes the two-framework case.
